runs.explanation: Line 58 divides by `window` (the requested window size) instead of `len(tail)` (the actual number of samples extracted). When fewer samples than `window` are available, this yields an incorrectly deflated mean rather than the true arithmetic mean of the available data.
runs.raw:
The bug is on line 58: `mean = total / window` divides by the fixed `window` size rather than by the actual number of samples in `tail` (`len(tail)`). When fewer samples than `window` are available (e.g., at the start of a series), this produces an incorrect (too-small) mean instead of the true arithmetic mean of the available samples.
